{{Quote|Luke Skywalker had done the impossible; the Death Star was destroyed. But the Empire would not take such a blow lightly. The counter attack on Yavin 4 hit us like lightning. The Alliance would have to evacuate, or be wiped out.|X2|<ref name="BF:ES" />}}
The '''Evacuation of Yavin''' was a battle between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] that took place six months after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. It was to be the final clash of the long [[Yavin blockade|siege]] of the moon before the Alliance base was evacuated.

==Prelude==
On what was later marked as the start of a new calendar system by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroyed]] the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] near their base on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="NEC" /> Although the Rebels [[Royal Award Ceremony/Legends|celebrated]] the destruction of the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]],<ref name="SWE4">{{Film|IV}}</ref> they prepared to evacuate from their base. The Empire [[Yavin blockade|blockaded]] the system, though. Small [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were able to escape the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]], and Rebel leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] undertook several missions on behalf of the Rebellion.<ref name="NEC" /> Before the blockade was in place, heavy equipment was taken away from [[Great Temple/Legends|Yavin base]], although the base's [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], concluded that the personnel could be evacuated quickly, and so convinced the Rebellion's leader [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] to allow the base to remain in operation as an act of defiance. The Imperial leadership therefore faced a dilemma with Yavin base still in operation. They could concentrate on wiping out the base, and risk a devastating raid elsewhere by the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]; or they could not focus heavily enough on blockading the system, and allow Mothma's forces to break the blockade. The blockade was therefore formed from a hastily-assembled task force from the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] and requisitioned from [[Nox Vellam]], [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] of the [[Bright Jewel Oversector/Legends|Bright Jewel Oversector]], which contained the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]].<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

During the blockade, Yavin 4 was the site of [[Space Battle at Yavin 4|several]]<ref name="FOC">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref> [[Rescue mission on Yavin 4|battles]]<ref name="SW:FC">''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]]''</ref> and [[Strike on Yavin 4|retaliatory strikes]].<ref name="BF II">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref> The Rebels also fought against small raiding parties.<ref name="MSW26">[[Star Wars (1977) 26|''Star Wars'' (1977) 26]]</ref> The blockade was successful in catching several Rebel units.<ref name="SW:GB" /> The blockade continued for six months, angering Imperial [[Imperial Navy/Legends|fleet]] commanders, although the Supreme Commander and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] held off from ordering Yavin base's destruction while construction continued on his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. When it was finished, it launched toward the blockade.<ref name="NEC" /> The Rebels used a [[power gem]] mounted on an [[Power-gem attack ship|attack ship]] piloted by [[Vrad Dodonna]] to delay the flagship. The move succeeded, but with the loss of Dodonna.<ref name="Doom Mission" /> The main portion of the Alliance Fleet was not at Yavin, though, and was able to stage a diversion to attempt to break the blockade.<ref name="NEC" />

==The battle==
===Defending the temple===
{{Quote|The surprise is mine, bucketheads!|Jan Dodonna before the destruction of the main base|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
[[File:Juggernautyavin.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Imperial forces on Yavin 4.]]
Rebel forces under Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] engaged Imperial ships [[Battle of Kli'aar|at]] [[Battle of Pinoora|three]] [[Battle of the Vallusk Cluster|locations]]—[[Kli'aar]], [[Pinoora]], and the [[Vallusk Cluster/Legends|Vallusk Cluster]]—in order to trick the Imperial blockade into diverting ships to reinforce the attacked positions, leaving a space open for the Rebel fleet to go into hyperspace.<ref name="Race for Survival" />

However, the completed ''Executor'' finally arrived at Yavin 4 with Lord Vader's [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] fleet, as well as elements of the personal fleet of Baron Tagge, and the battle began.<ref name="EGW" /> [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] and [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] on [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bikes]] scoured the moon for stragglers.<ref name="SW:GB" />

[[File:Yavin_base_destroyed.jpg|thumb|right|A Rebel base destroyed.]]
Survivors of the Death Star combined their ranks and moved against the Rebel's [[Great Temple/Legends|main base]]. Imperial [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]], [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], [[Imperial shock trooper/Legends|shock troopers]], [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]], and [[Vehicle Command Crewer|Imperial Army pilots]] fought against [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]], [[Rebel Marksman/Legends|snipers]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] soldiers, and [[Rebel Vanguard/Legends|vanguard divisions]] around a set of ruins and a Fountain near the base. The Rebels defeated those forces, buying time for the evacuation.<ref name="BF I" /> General Dodonna ordered the base's fighters and transports to escape though he refused to evacuate himself until everyone had left.<ref name="NEC" />

During the assault, the Empire launched an array of vehicles including [[HAVw A5 Juggernaut|A5 Juggernauts]].<ref name="Yavin TCG" />
The Rebel operatives [[X2]] and [[Shara]] fought against Imperial forces during the battle. X2 helped destroy a [[TX-130T fighter tank]], and went into the base to activate the evacuation signal. A TIE bomber blew the comm tower, though, and X2 was forced activate turrets to destroy the bombers, then went out to repair the tower. X2 took a [[T4-B heavy tank]] to make his way to the waterfall. He then used his [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] to jump to the top of the tower and used a [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusioncutter]] to repair the tower. He then took control of an anti-air gun to defend the tower from more bombers. X2 then retreated to the hangar and took an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]].<ref name="BF:ES" />

[[File:TIEBombersattack.jpg|thumb|left|250px|TIE Bombers attacking the Rebel base]]
Lord Vader personally led a force of stormtroopers and [[Dewback trooper/Legends|dewback troopers]] in an attack on the main Rebel base, but he was caught in a fight with the Rebel forces from two other bases, [[Falcon Base]] and [[Raven Base]]. Although he was harassed by scouts on [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]], he proceeded to destroy both outposts, which were defended by troopers, [[Tauntaun Trooper|cavalry units]], and [[T1-B hovertank]]s. After eliminating the Rebel [[Barracks/Legends|troop centers]] and [[Factory/Legends|Mech factories]], he was supplied with reinforcements, including 2 AT-STs and TIE bombers. The force pushed all the way up to the Rebel's [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Temple]] and destroyed the [[Air defense tower|missile turrets]] to allow for an aerial bombardment. Vader commented that they had a "special surprise" waiting for when the air defenses were down.<ref name="SW:GB" />

===Evacuating===
{{Quote|Keep moving, commander. We will need to eliminate any lingering rebels.|Darth Vader|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
After the evacuation order had been given, a smaller Imperial force had pushed their way through to the Massassi Temple. However, Commander [[Col Serra]] and his newly created fighting force, [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]], pushed back the Imperials and set up a perimeter to keep the Imperials busy while the evacuation proceeded. The door to the temple jammed, so a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]-equipped Squadron member jumped through a hole and repaired the door, as well as a turret to defend the temple. Another TX-130T fighter tank then arrived, and Serra ordered the squadron to destroy it. The group then captured a position near the Pyramid, killing at least four stormtroopers. A scout trooper managed to steal communication logs from the Temple, and the Squadron hunted the trooper and recovered the logs. Serra then ordered the Squadron to destroy three Data Banks with sensitive information to prevent them from falling into Imperial hands before escaping.<ref name="BF:RS" />

General Dodonna and Lieutenant [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]] found themselves trapped inside the Massassi temple as Imperial ground forces occupied the base; they were extracted by [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)|Red Squadron's]] [[Luke Skywalker]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Sarkli]] Piett.<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> Dodonna, however, refused to board an escape transport: he had planted several concussion charges,<ref name="NEC" /> which decimated a whole squadron of [[TIE/ca Punisher/Legends|TIE Interdictors]] as they attacked the Temple.<ref name="SW:GB" /> It was believed that the General had died in action, though it was later revealed he had been captured and imprisoned in the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] and [[Prison/Legends|prison]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]''.<ref name="NEC" /> A mind-scan conducted on the captive officer revealed the Rebel's plan to [[Reytha campaign|attack]] the [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] [[Reytha]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

Over the moon, X2 joined with Commander Serra and took out a communications array on a Star Destroyer to prevent reinforcements from being called in. They then boarded the ship, and X2 sabotaged the [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. The two escaped before the ship blew.<ref name="BF:ES" />

[[File:GriffMeetsExecutor-CSW.png|thumb|left|250px|Griff's fate]]
The evacuated Rebels rendezvoused in orbit to run the blockade. Vader, on-board his flagship, deliberately left a weak spot in the blockade to try to trap [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the pilot who had destroyed the Death Star. The Rebel fleet moved within range of the ''Executor'', but [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Amise Griff]], commanding three Star Destroyers, made a hyperspace jump to cover the hole, and his three starships crashed into Vader's flagship, allowing the Rebels an instant in which to escape into hyperspace.<ref name="Race for Survival" />

Meanwhile, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], having been reassigned from the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] to the [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] to aid in the evacuation of the Rebels, was commissioned to fly an [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]]<ref name="A/R-22" /> under the call sign of ''Red 1'' to escort the five CR90 Corvette group [[Crescent (corvette group)|''Crescent'']], which had been carrying the last of the evacuated staff from Yavin.<ref name="X-Wing" /> Upon arriving from hyperspace to the Frigate's rendezvous point, while verifying the location of the corvettes from his inflight map, Farlander noticed a squadron of Imperial [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|''Alpha''-class Xg-1 Star Wing]] Assault Gunboats attacking a group of four supply freighters 16 kilometers away. As he was too far away from them to save them in time (due to their shields already failing) as well as his immediate orders being to guard the corvettes, he let them be destroyed. He then reviewed his mission start-up procedures and cycled through the corvettes to inspect their contents, before assigning the lead ship to memory. Farlander then increased his own shields to maximum, just in time for the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B Frigate]] ''[[Red Wind]]'' exit hyperspace from 10 kilometers away. The ''Red Wind'' then proceeded to launch two TIE/LN starfighters from Eta squadron, and four TIE/sa bombers. Farlander then went after the TIE Bombers, also intercepting several TIE fighters (including doing hull damage to the TIE fighter ''Eta 1''). He kept his focus on the TIE bombers, viewing them as the priority threat. Afterwards, the ''Red Wind'' launched Beta Squadron of TIE Bombers. He also had to evade the ''Red Wind''{{'s}} gunners while tailing the bombers, due to being in close proximity to the frigate. Farlander then continued to defend Rebel transports as they reached one hyperspace jump point, and was eventually assisted by the arrival of [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]]<ref name="Strategy Guide">''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>

==Aftermath==
During the blockade, Skywalker had discovered an ice world called [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. He suggested that a command center for the Alliance could be established there if necessary.<ref name="GG3">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> The evacuated Rebels hoped to establish a base on Hoth, but they had to replenish their supplies first.<ref name="NEC" /> After escaping the blockade, the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]] were forced to battle the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Hungry Ghost]]''.<ref name="Death Star Pirates" /> [[Renegade Squadron/Legends|Renegade Squadron]] [[Battle of the Graveyard|searched]] for a new base.<ref name="BF:RS" /> After his failure to capture the Rebel leadership, Vader continued his search for Skywalker.<ref name="SW:GB" /> In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], the Rebels were able to take refuge on Hoth, beginning the construction of a [[Echo Base/Legends|new base]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Expandsect}}
{{Quote|The destruction of the Death Star is a tremendous victory, but several Imperial garrisons escaped the blast and their escape pods have crashed on the planet. They have combined their ranks and are moving on our base to attack. We must finish off the Imperials before our celebration can truly begin.|Admiral Ackbar briefing the player before the mission|<ref name="BF I" />}}
The evacuation of Yavin was first mentioned in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], and appeared in the ''[[Doom Mission]]''<ref name="Doom Mission" /> and ''[[Race for Survival]]'' comic strips in [[1983]].<ref name="Race for Survival" /> Other sources released since then expanded on the battle.

In the Darth Vader campaign [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', Mara Jade can be spotted on the game map during the battle. After she is spotted, Vader exclaims "What's this?!" and Jade leaves on a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]. However, since it is required to use cheat codes to see her, her appearance is likely an easter-egg and its [[canon]] status is dubious. In addition, the same game also implies that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] killed [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] shortly after learning about Rebel activities on Raythar. Also, if the [[TIE/ca Punisher/Legends|TIE Interdictors]] are moved far enough from the temple when it explodes, they will not be destroyed.<ref name="SW:GB" />

One source which includes the evacuation is the [[2004]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'', although the storyline is considered S-canon by [[Leland Chee]], the Keeper of the [[Lucasfilm]] [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Blog-holocron|56">{{Blog|holocron|56|starwars.com forum HolocronKeeper comments archive}}</ref> The battle also appears in the game's [[2009]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'', although only in the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] version.<ref name="BF:ES" />

A seeming continuity error was created by ''[[Imperial Spy]]'', which placed [[Q-7N]] at the Evacuation of Yavin and the preceding evacuation preparation. This clashes with [[Star Wars Missions 20: Total Destruction|''Star Wars Missions 20: Total Destruction'']], which shows Q-7N's destruction, and, subsequently, the Rebels returning to Yavin Four. However, in Imperial Spy, Q-7N does not appear in the last set of frames, which show the Rebels after the evacuation. It is possible that the events of the final Missions arc took place during the events of Imperial Spy, though this has not been confirmed. However, it is also possible (but unlikly) that [[Imperial Spy]] shows an entirely different event, after which the Rebels returned to Yavin.
